revision.text: * {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|canonical_name_get|Language|id=csdoc_language}}: The language (or a comma-separated list of the languages) in which the source is written, as either the [[ISO 639]] language code (preferred) or the full language name. Examples: {{para|language|ru}}; {{para|lang|fr, pt-br}}; {{Para|lang|Russian}}; {{para|language|French, Portuguese}}. [[Template:Citation Style documentation/language/doc|See the list of supported codes and names]]. Do not use templates or wikilinks. Displays in parentheses with "in" before the language name or names. When the only source language is English, no language is displayed in the citation. The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Aliases: {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|alias_names_get|Language}}

* {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|canonical_name_get|Language|id=csdoc_language}}: The language (or a comma-separated list of the languages) in which the source is written, as either the [[ISO 639]] language code (preferred) or the full language name. Examples: {{para|language|ru}}; {{para|lang|fr, pt-br}}; {{Para|lang|Russian}}; {{para|language|French, Portuguese}}. [[Template:Citation Style documentation/language/doc|See the list of supported codes and names]]. Do not use templates or wikilinks. Displays in parentheses with "in" before the language name or names. When the only source language is English, no language is displayed in the citation. The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Aliases: {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|alias_names_get|Language}}

Examples: {{para|language|ru}}; {{para|lang|fr, pt-br}}; {{Para|lang|Russian}}; {{para|language|French, Portuguese}}. [[Template:Citation Style documentation/language/doc|See the list of supported codes and names]]. Do not use templates or wikilinks. Displays in parentheses with "in" before the language name or names. When the only source language is English, no language is displayed in the citation. The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Aliases: {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|alias_names_get|Language}}

The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""

The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23168229401|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: * {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|canonical_name_get|Language|id=csdoc_language}}: The language (or a comma-separated list of the languages) in which the source is written, as either the [[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code (preferred) or the full language name. [[:en:Template:Citation Style documentation/language/doc|See the list of supported codes and names]]. Do not use templates or wikilinks. Displays in parentheses with "in" before the language name or names. When the only source language is English, no language is displayed in the citation. The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Aliases: {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|alias_names_get|Language}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

[[:en:Template:Citation Style documentation/language/doc|See the list of supported codes and names]]. Do not use templates or wikilinks. Displays in parentheses with "in" before the language name or names. When the only source language is English, no language is displayed in the citation. The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Citation_Style_documentation%2Flanguage here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: * {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|canonical_name_get|Language|id=csdoc_language}}: The language (or a comma-separated list of the languages) in which the source is written, as either the [[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code (preferred) or the full language name. Examples: {{para|language|ru}}; {{para|lang|fr, pt-br}}; {{Para|lang|Russian}}; {{para|language|French, Portuguese}}. [[:en:Template:Citation Style documentation/language/doc|See the list of supported codes and names]]. Do not use templates or wikilinks. Displays in parentheses with "in" before the language name or names. When the only source language is English, no language is displayed in the citation. The use of languages recognized by the citation module adds the page to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|CS1 foreign language sources}}. Because cs1|2 templates are often copied from en.wiki to other wikis, the use of language codes is preferred so that language names render in the correct language and form, e.g. {{lang|fr|{{#language:es|fr}}}} at a French-language wiki instead of the English word "Spanish". Aliases: {{#invoke:cs1 documentation support|alias_names_get|Language}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
